place: Westwood borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Westwood’s first railroad station opened in 1870, sparking a boom that turned the borough into a bustling railroad suburb.
The Bogert family, once a leading local dynasty, still lends its name to streets and landmarks across Westwood.
Westwood’s historic train station now houses a museum that opens every second Saturday, inviting visitors to explore local heritage.
In the 1880s, Westwood boasted four factories, a distillery, a new school, and a Reformed Church, reflecting its rapid industrial growth.
The borough’s first police department was founded in September 1894, just months after Westwood’s official establishment.
Westwood’s heritage society preserves the town’s story through a collection of photographs, artifacts, and oral histories, encouraging community participation.
